# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-microservice-deployment
  labels:
    app: nodejs-microservice
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nodejs-microservice
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nodejs-microservice
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nodejs-microservice
          image: your-repo/your-nodejs-app:latest # Replace with your actual image
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000 # Assuming your Node.js app listens on port 3000
              name: http
          env:
            - name: MONGO_URI # Environment variable for your MongoDB connection string
              value: "mongodb://mongodb-service:27017/yourdatabase" # Replace with your MongoDB connection string (e.g., pointing to a MongoDB Service)
            - name: PORT
              value: "3000" # Expose the container port as an environment variable
